concerns.text: Task T022 implements a sensitivity sweep for the instability threshold but describes sweeping 'across a range of low-magnitude values' without specifying the exact discrete values {0.25, 0.30, 0.35} mandated by spec.md SC-005. This ambiguity risks failing the specific testability criterion defined in the spec.

Tasks T021a and T029a mandate updating spec.md to replace 'retention fraction' with 'Vortex Density' and 'One-Way ANOVA' with 'Two-Way ANOVA'. However, the current spec.md (FR-004, FR-005, SC-001, SC-003) still contains the original invalid requirements. The tasks create a circular dependency where implementation cannot legally proceed without first modifying the source document that defines the scope, and no task exists to formally amend FR-001 regarding the grid size (256x256 vs 64x64) which is also a critical mismatch.

Task T029 implements 'Dunnett's post-hoc test' as per the plan, but Spec FR-005 explicitly mandates a 'one-sample t-test' and 'one-way ANOVA' without mentioning post-hoc tests. While the plan corrects this, there is no corresponding task to formally remove the 'one-sample t-test' requirement from FR-005, leaving the spec text legally requiring a test that the code will not implement.
